---
title: "Week 4: Solutions Guide"
subtitle: "Complete Solutions for Lab and Assessments"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
---

# Lab Solutions

## Part 1: String Slicing Mastery

### Exercise 1.1: Basic Slicing
```python
text = "Python Programming"

# Solutions:
first_word = text[0:6]      # "Python"
second_word = text[7:18]    # "Programming" or text[7:]
first_three = text[0:3]     # "Pyt" or text[:3]
last_four = text[14:18]     # "ming" or text[-4:]
middle_five = text[5:10]    # "n Pro"
```

### Exercise 1.2: Negative Indexing
```python
email = "student@university.edu"

# Solutions:
domain = email[8:]          # "university.edu" or email[-14:]
username = email[:7]        # "student" or email[:-15]
extension = email[-4:]      # ".edu"
without_ext = email[:-4]    # "student@university"
```

### Exercise 1.3: Step Parameter
```python
alphabet = "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ"

# Solutions:
every_second = alphabet[::2]     # "ACEGIKMOQSUWY"
every_third = alphabet[::3]      # "ADGJMPSVY"
reversed_string = alphabet[::-1] # "ZYXWVUTSRQPONMLKJIHGFEDCBA"
every_second_back = alphabet[::-2] # "ZXVTRPNLJHFDB"
```

### Exercise 1.4: Practical Slicing
```python
# Filename extraction
filename = "report_2024_final.pdf"
name = filename[0:17]       # "report_2024_final" or filename[:-4]
extension = filename[-3:]   # "pdf" or filename[18:]
year = filename[7:11]       # "2024"

# Phone number extraction
phone = "(555) 123-4567"
area_code = phone[1:4]      # "555"
first_part = phone[6:9]     # "123"
last_part = phone[10:]     # "4567" or phone[-4:]

# URL extraction
url = "https://www.example.com/page"
protocol = url[0:5]         # "https" or url[:5]
# Find domain dynamically
start = url.find("//") + 2
end = url.find("/", start)
domain = url[start:end]     # "www.example.com"
```

## Part 2: String Methods Practice

### Exercise 2.1: Case Methods
```python
name1 = "john doe"
name2 = "JANE SMITH"
name3 = "mArY jOhNsOn"

clean1 = name1.title()              # "John Doe"
clean2 = name2.lower().title()      # "Jane Smith"
clean3 = name3.lower().title()      # "Mary Johnson"

# Case-insensitive comparison
user_input = "YeS"
if user_input.lower() == "yes":
    print("User agreed!")
```

### Exercise 2.2: Whitespace Cleaning
```python
input1 = "  hello  "
input2 = "\tPython\n"
input3 = "  multiple   spaces  here  "

clean1 = input1.strip()             # "hello"
clean2 = input2.strip()             # "Python"

# Remove multiple spaces
clean3 = input3.strip()
while "  " in clean3:
    clean3 = clean3.replace("  ", " ")
# Result: "multiple spaces here"
```

### Exercise 2.3: Search and Replace
```python
text = "I love programming in Java. Java is great!"

# 1. Check if "Java" is in text
if "Java" in text:
    print("Found Java")

# 2. Find position
pos = text.find("Java")             # 22

# 3. Count occurrences
count = text.count("Java")          # 2

# 4. Replace all
python_text = text.replace("Java", "Python")

# 5. Replace first only
first_only = text.replace("Java", "Python", 1)
```

### Exercise 2.4: Method Chaining
```python
# Clean email
email = "  USER@GMAIL.COM  "
clean_email = email.strip().lower() # "user@gmail.com"

# Clean name with spaces
name = "  john    doe  "
clean_name = " ".join(name.strip().split()).title()  # "John Doe"

# Extract and clean domain
messy_email = "  Student@UNIVERSITY.EDU  \n"
domain = messy_email.strip().lower().split("@")[1]  # "university.edu"
```

## Part 3: Split and Join Operations

### Exercise 3.1: Basic Splitting
```python
# CSV data
csv_data = "John,25,Engineer"
fields = csv_data.split(",")
name = fields[0]    # "John"
age = fields[1]     # "25"
job = fields[2]     # "Engineer"

# Phone parts
phone = "555-123-4567"
parts = phone.split("-")  # ['555', '123', '4567']

# Limited split
text = "one-two-three-four-five"
first_three = text.split("-", 2)  # ['one', 'two', 'three-four-five']

# Whitespace split
sentence = "  Python   is    awesome  "
words = sentence.split()  # ['Python', 'is', 'awesome']
```

### Exercise 3.2: Joining Lists
```python
words = ["Python", "is", "fun"]
sentence = " ".join(words)          # "Python is fun"

items = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
comma_list = ",".join(items)        # "apple,banana,cherry"
nice_list = ", ".join(items)        # "apple, banana, cherry"
bullet_list = "\n- ".join(items)    # "apple\n- banana\n- cherry"

# Formatted message
data = ["John", "25", "Engineer"]
formatted = " | ".join([f"Name: {data[0]}", f"Age: {data[1]}", f"Job: {data[2]}"])
```

### Exercise 3.3: Parse and Rebuild
```python
email_string = "john@GMAIL.com; ALICE@yahoo.COM;bob@HOTMAIL.com"

# Split
emails = email_string.split(";")

# Clean each
clean_emails = []
for email in emails:
    clean = email.strip().lower()
    clean_emails.append(clean)

# Join back
clean_string = "; ".join(clean_emails)
# Result: "john@gmail.com; alice@yahoo.com; bob@hotmail.com"
```

---

# Word Game Solutions

## Word Scrambler - Complete Implementation

```python
"""
Word Scrambler Game
A simple word guessing game with scrambled words
"""

print("=== Word Scrambler Game ===")
print("Unscramble the words to score points!\n")

# Word list by difficulty
easy_words = ["cat", "dog", "run", "big", "red"]
medium_words = ["python", "program", "computer", "science"]
hard_words = ["algorithm", "development", "application", "technology"]

# Game setup
score = 0
rounds = 5
difficulty = input("Choose difficulty (easy/medium/hard): ").lower()

# Select word list
if difficulty == "easy":
    words = easy_words
elif difficulty == "hard":
    words = hard_words
else:
    words = medium_words
    difficulty = "medium"

print(f"\nStarting {difficulty} mode with {rounds} rounds...\n")

# Game loop
for round_num in range(1, rounds + 1):
    # Get word for round
    word_index = (round_num - 1) % len(words)
    original = words[word_index]
    
    # Scramble by swapping adjacent pairs
    scrambled = ""
    for i in range(0, len(original) - 1, 2):
        scrambled = scrambled + original[i+1] + original[i]
    if len(original) % 2 == 1:
        scrambled = scrambled + original[-1]
    
    # If scramble didn't work well, reverse
    if scrambled == original:
        scrambled = original[::-1]
    
    print(f"Round {round_num}/{rounds}")
    print(f"Scrambled word: {scrambled}")
    print(f"Hint: {len(original)} letters")
    
    # Get guesses
    attempts = 3
    guessed = False
    
    while attempts > 0 and not guessed:
        guess = input(f"\nYour guess ({attempts} attempts left): ").strip().lower()
        
        if not guess:
            print("Please enter a word!")
            continue
            
        if guess == original:
            points = attempts * 10  # More points for fewer attempts
            score = score + points
            print(f"Correct! +{points} points")
            guessed = True
        else:
            attempts = attempts - 1
            if attempts > 0:
                print("Wrong! Try again.")
                # Give hints
                if attempts == 2:
                    print(f"Hint: First letter is '{original[0]}'")
                elif attempts == 1:
                    print(f"Hint: Last letter is '{original[-1]}'")
    
    if not guessed:
        print(f"Out of attempts! The word was: {original}")
    
    print(f"Current score: {score}\n")
    print("-" * 40)

# Game over
print(f"\nGame Over!")
print(f"Final Score: {score}/{rounds * 30} possible points")

# Rating
percentage = (score / (rounds * 30)) * 100
if percentage >= 80:
    print("Excellent! You're a word master!")
elif percentage >= 60:
    print("Great job! You did well!")
elif percentage >= 40:
    print("Not bad! Keep practicing!")
else:
    print("Nice try! Play again to improve!")
```

## Hangman - Simple Implementation

```python
"""
Simple Hangman Game
Guess the word one letter at a time
"""

print("=== Hangman Game ===")
print("Guess the word one letter at a time!\n")

# Word categories
animals = ["elephant", "giraffe", "penguin", "dolphin"]
foods = ["pizza", "burger", "pasta", "sushi"]

# Choose category
print("Categories: animals, food")
category = input("Choose category: ").lower()

if category == "animals":
    words = animals
else:
    words = foods
    category = "food"

# Select word
import random  # Just for word selection
word = words[0]  # Without random, just use first

# Game setup
guessed_letters = []
max_wrong = 6
wrong_guesses = 0

print(f"\nCategory: {category}")
print(f"Word has {len(word)} letters")

# Game loop
while wrong_guesses < max_wrong:
    # Display word state
    display = ""
    for letter in word:
        if letter in guessed_letters:
            display = display + letter + " "
        else:
            display = display + "_ "
    
    print(f"\nWord: {display}")
    print(f"Guessed: {', '.join(guessed_letters)}")
    print(f"Wrong guesses: {wrong_guesses}/{max_wrong}")
    
    # Check if won
    if "_" not in display:
        print("\nCongratulations! You won!")
        print(f"The word was: {word}")
        break
    
    # Get guess
    guess = input("\nGuess a letter: ").lower().strip()
    
    # Validate
    if not guess:
        print("Please enter a letter!")
        continue
    if len(guess) != 1:
        print("One letter at a time!")
        continue
    if not guess.isalpha():
        print("Letters only!")
        continue
    if guess in guessed_letters:
        print("Already guessed that!")
        continue
    
    # Process guess
    guessed_letters.append(guess)
    
    if guess in word:
        count = word.count(guess)
        print(f"Good! '{guess}' appears {count} time(s)")
    else:
        wrong_guesses = wrong_guesses + 1
        print(f"Sorry, no '{guess}'")
        
        # Show hangman state
        if wrong_guesses == 1:
            print("   O")
        elif wrong_guesses == 2:
            print("   O\n   |")
        elif wrong_guesses == 3:
            print("   O\n  /|")
        elif wrong_guesses == 4:
            print("   O\n  /|\\")
        elif wrong_guesses == 5:
            print("   O\n  /|\\\n  /")
        elif wrong_guesses == 6:
            print("   O\n  /|\\\n  / \\")

# Game over
if wrong_guesses >= max_wrong:
    print("\nGame Over! You lose!")
    print(f"The word was: {word}")
```

---

# Assessment Solutions

## Slicing Mastery Assessment

### Part 1: Basic String Slicing
1. `text[0:6]` or `text[:6]`
2. `text[7:18]` or `text[7:]`
3. `text[2:6]`
4. `text[11:15]`
5. `text[0:4]` or `text[:4]`
6. `text[13:18]` or `text[-5:]`

### Part 2: Negative Indexing
1. `email[-4:]`
2. `email[:-4]`
3. `email[13:]` or `email[-14:]`
4. `email[:12]`

### Part 3: Step Parameter
1. `alphabet[::2]` or `alphabet[0::2]`
2. `alphabet[::3]` or `alphabet[0::3]`
3. `alphabet[::-1]`
4. `alphabet[5:10:2]`

### Part 5: Practical Slicing
```python
# Problem 1
name_part = filename[0:20] or filename[:-4]
extension = filename[-3:] or filename[21:]
year = filename[7:11]
quarter = filename[12:14]

# Problem 2
area_code = phone[1:4]
main_number = phone[6:14]
extension = phone[-3:]

# Problem 3
protocol = url[0:5]
domain = url[8:23]
path = url[23:]
```

## String Methods Assessment

### Part 1: Method Selection
1. `.upper()`
2. `.strip()`
3. `.find("@")`
4. `.replace("old", "new")`
5. `.isalpha()`
6. `.split(",")`
7. `.title()` or `.capitalize()`
8. `.count("a")`
9. `.startswith("http")`
10. `"-".join()`

### Part 2: Method Application
```python
# Problem 1
clean_name = name.strip().title()
final_name = " ".join(clean_name.split())

# Problem 2
clean_email = email.strip().lower()
has_at = "@" in clean_email
has_dot = "." in clean_email
at_pos = clean_email.find("@")
dot_after_at = "." in clean_email[at_pos:]

# Problem 3
long_enough = len(password) >= 8
has_upper = any(c.isupper() for c in password)
has_lower = any(c.islower() for c in password)
has_digit = any(c.isdigit() for c in password)
```

### Part 5: Error Detection
```python
# Fix 1
name = name.title()

# Fix 2
if email.lower() == "user@domain.com":

# Fix 3
clean = text.strip()

# Fix 4
list = words.split(",")
```

---

# Teaching Notes

## Common Patterns to Emphasize

1. **Input Cleaning Pipeline**:
   ```python
   # Always: strip → lower/title → validate
   user_input = input("Enter: ").strip().lower()
   ```

2. **Safe Slicing**:
   ```python
   # Check length before slicing
   if len(text) >= 3:
       last_three = text[-3:]
   ```

3. **Method Chaining Order**:
   ```python
   # Strip THEN transform
   clean = text.strip().title()
   ```

## Debugging Tips for Students

1. Print intermediate results
2. Check string length before slicing
3. Remember strings are immutable
4. Test with edge cases (empty, single char)
5. Visualize indices on paper
